title: astragalus idaeus
intro: Astragalus idaeus, also known as the Mongolian milkvetch or Mongolian astragalus, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to provide various health benefits, such as enhancing immune function, reducing fatigue, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory diseases like bronchitis and asthma. Astragalus idaeus is typically consumed in the form of herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine preparations.
